food_subgroup: Cheese-based foam topping / dairy dessert topping

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for salted cheese foam topping. FOODON_03301116 (cheese food product) is the nearest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for whipped cheese foam topping. A00LC (Cheese, generic) is the closest defensible parent. Facet descriptors for 'whipped' and 'topping' would apply in a full FoodEx2 encoding.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 102263004 (Cheese substance) is the closest parent. No specific concept exists for salted cheese foam topping.

serving.description: 1 portion of cheese foam topping (approx. 60 g)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of HEYTEA Cheese Cloud Original is approximately 60 g of salted whipped cheese foam, typically consumed as a topping layer sipped through the rim of a tea or beverage cup.

nutrient_source.title: Cream cheese, full fat — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 01017); Whipping cream — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 01053); adapted composite for salted whipped cheese foam
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for HEYTEA Cheese Cloud Original exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA Branded Foods, or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for cream cheese (NDB 01017, ~55% by weight), fresh whole milk (NDB 01077, ~25%), and heavy whipping cream (NDB 01053, ~15%), with salt (~1%) and sugar (~4%) adjustments. Energy calculated via Atwater factors. Values represent a plausible central estimate; actual product formulation may differ. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of dairy foods — cream cheese and full-fat dairy analogues; University of Sydney GI Database reference for full-fat dairy products (GI ~27)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for HEYTEA Cheese Cloud Original. GI of 27 is an analogous estimate based on full-fat dairy products (cream cheese, whole milk, cream) as listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. Full-fat dairy consistently falls in the low-GI range (GI 15–40) due to high fat and protein content slowing glucose absorption. Glycemic load per 60 g serving = (27 × 7.8 × 0.60) / 100 = 1.26, rounded to 1.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg/100 g) and saturated fat
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size of this cheese foam topping, as a 60 g serving contributes approximately 186 mg sodium; those on a sodium-restricted diet (<1500 mg/day) should account for this contribution within their daily allowance.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~27) and low carbohydrate load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low GI (~27) and low glycemic load (~1.3 per 60 g serving) make this cheese foam topping unlikely to cause rapid postprandial glucose spikes; however, the high saturated fat content warrants moderation given its association with insulin resistance in the context of a high-fat diet.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~15.4 g/100 g) and cholesterol (~82 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: A 60 g serving provides approximately 9.2 g of saturated fat, which represents a substantial proportion of the recommended daily limit of <20 g for adults; individuals with cardiovascular disease or dyslipidaemia should consume this product sparingly and within a diet low in total saturated fat.

llm_training.natural_language_description: HEYTEA Cheese Cloud (Original) is a salted whipped cheese foam topping developed by the Chinese tea chain HEYTEA, served at their Marina Bay Sands outlet in Singapore. The product is made from real dairy ingredients — primarily cream cheese, fresh whole milk, and whipping cream — whipped to a light, airy, cloud-like consistency and lightly salted. It is not made with non-dairy creamers. The foam is typically consumed as a topping layer on tea-based beverages, sipped through the rim of the cup so that the savoury-sweet cheese foam mingles with the tea beneath. The texture is smooth, creamy, and mousse-like, with a mildly salty and lightly sweet flavour profile that contrasts pleasantly with the bitterness of tea. Nutritionally, the topping is calorie-dense (~270 kcal/100 g) due to its high fat content (~24.5 g/100 g), predominantly saturated fat (~15.4 g/100 g). Carbohydrate content is low (~7.8 g/100 g) and the glycemic index is estimated at approximately 27 (low GI), consistent with full-fat dairy products. Sodium content is moderate (~310 mg/100 g), reflecting the salted formulation. The product is suitable for lacto-vegetarians but is not vegan, not dairy-free, and halal status is unconfirmed. It is relevant to clinical dietary counselling for cardiovascular disease and hypertension due to its saturated fat and sodium content, and is relatively safe for blood glucose management due to its low GI and low glycemic load per serving.
common_user_queries: How many calories in HEYTEA cheese foam?
Is cheese cloud suitable for diabetics?
What is cheese foam made of?
How much sodium in cheese foam topping?
What is the GI of cheese foam?
Is HEYTEA cheese cloud gluten free?

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry for HEYTEA Cheese Cloud Original exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA Branded Foods, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for cream cheese, whole milk, and heavy whipping cream. The GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database full-fat dairy references. Confidence is medium rather than low because the ingredient composition is well-characterised (real dairy, no creamers) and the USDA SR Legacy source data is high quality.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA SR Legacy (cream cheese NDB 01017, whole milk NDB 01077, heavy whipping cream NDB 01053) weighted approximately 55:25:15 with salt and sugar adjustments. Actual HEYTEA product formulation is proprietary and may differ. 2. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero): cheese foam contains no plant-derived fibre; this is naturally zero for a pure dairy foam product. 3. GI of 27 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database full-fat dairy category; no direct measurement exists for this product. Glycemic load per 60 g serving = 1.3. 4. Halal status is not confirmed from an approved source; the is_halal flag is set to false pending verification. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all estimated from the USDA SR Legacy composite; no sentinel -1 values are used as the composite source provides plausible estimates for all fields. 6. This food is classified under Dessert cuisine for multilingual purposes; mandatory Singaporean multilingual fields (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are populated because the product is sold in Singapore and the cuisine tags include Dessert and Bubble Tea, which are treated as Singaporean-context dishes per Project Veritas v2 multilingual rules.

Iteration 1: Populated all four mandatory Singaporean multilingual fields (Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as product is sold in Singapore.
Iteration 1: Set GI to 27 (analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database full-fat dairy) with GL = 1.3 per 60 g serving.
Iteration 1: Verified aliases_by_language consistency — all entries present verbatim in flat aliases list.
Iteration 1: Confirmed dietary_fibre_g = 0.0 is naturally zero for pure dairy foam; documented in notes.
Iteration 2: Verified all ontology match_type values use only allowed strings (exact, closest_parent, none). Confirmed no invented codes. Self-score confirmed 8/8 PASS.
